title: Koneswarar Temple, Kudavasal
tagline: Koneswarar Temple, Kudavasal — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Koneswarar Temple (Tamil: குடவாசல் கோணேசுவரர் கோயில்) is a Hindu temple in the town of Kudavasal in the Tiruvarur district of Tamil Nadu, India.
overview.description: Koneswarar Temple (Tamil: குடவாசல் கோணேசுவரர் கோயில்) is a Hindu temple in the town of Kudavasal in the Tiruvarur district of Tamil Nadu, India. The presiding deity is Shiva. Koneswarar Temple is situated at Tirukudavayil in Thanjavur District. Build before 660 AD The temple is dedicated to Lord Shiva.

topPlaces.name: Kudavasal taluk
topPlaces.description: Kudavasal taluk is a taluk in Tiruvarur district of the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The headquarters of the taluk is the town of Kudavasal.

topPlaces.name: Kudavasal
topPlaces.description: Kudavasal or Kodavasal is a panchayat in Thiruvarur district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The town is the headquarters of Kudavasal taluk.

topPlaces.name: Kudavasal block
topPlaces.description: Kudavasal block is a revenue block in the Kudavasal taluk of Tiruvarur district, Tamil Nadu, India. It has a total of 49 panchayat villages.

topPlaces.name: Thirumeignanam Gnanaparameswarar Temple
topPlaces.description: Thirumeignanam Gnanaparameswarar Temple (Tamil: திருமெய்ஞானம் ஞானபரமேஸ்வரர் கோயில்) is a Hindu temple located at Thirumeignanam in Thanjavur district, Tamil Nadu, India. The temple is dedicated to Shiva, as the moolavar presiding deity, in his manifestation as Gnanaparameswarar.

topPlaces.name: Adippuliyur
topPlaces.description: Adippuliyur is a village in the Kudavasal taluk of Tiruvarur district, Tamil Nadu, India.

topPlaces.name: Sengalipuram
topPlaces.description: Sengalipuram ("Shivakalipuram") is a village situated in Thiruvarur district in the south Indian state of Tamil Nadu Notable people from the village include Sengalipuram Muthanna, Anantharama Deekshithar and the Tamil writer "Thiru". It is one of the 18 Vathima villages.

topPlaces.name: Manjakkudi
topPlaces.description: Manjakkudi is a village on the banks of Kaveri river in Tiruvarur district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It comes under the Kudavasal Taluk.

topPlaces.name: Saranathan Temple
topPlaces.description: The Saranathan Temple in Thirucherai, a village on the outskirts of Kumbakonam in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu, is dedicated to the Hindu god Vishnu. The temple is glorified in the Nalayira Divya Prabandham, the early medieval Tamil canon of the Alvar saints from the 6th–9th centuries CE. It is one of the 108 Divya Desams dedicated to Vishnu, who is worshipped as Saranathan and his consort Lakshmi as Saranayaki.

items.note: Kudavasal taluk is a taluk in Tiruvarur district of the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The headquarters of the taluk is the town of Kudavasal.
Kudavasal or Kodavasal is a panchayat in Thiruvarur district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The town is the headquarters of Kudavasal taluk.
Kudavasal block is a revenue block in the Kudavasal taluk of Tiruvarur district, Tamil Nadu, India. It has a total of 49 panchayat villages.

Thirumeignanam Gnanaparameswarar Temple (Tamil: திருமெய்ஞானம் ஞானபரமேஸ்வரர் கோயில்) is a Hindu temple located at Thirumeignanam in Thanjavur district, Tamil Nadu, India. The temple is dedicated to Shiva, as the moolavar presiding deity, in his manifestation as Gnanaparameswarar.
Adippuliyur is a village in the Kudavasal taluk of Tiruvarur district, Tamil Nadu, India.

Sengalipuram ("Shivakalipuram") is a village situated in Thiruvarur district in the south Indian state of Tamil Nadu Notable people from the village include Sengalipuram Muthanna, Anantharama Deekshithar and the Tamil writer "Thiru". It is one of the 18 Vathima villages.
Manjakkudi is a village on the banks of Kaveri river in Tiruvarur district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It comes under the Kudavasal Taluk.

The Saranathan Temple in Thirucherai, a village on the outskirts of Kumbakonam in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu, is dedicated to the Hindu god Vishnu. The temple is glorified in the Nalayira Divya Prabandham, the early medieval Tamil canon of the Alvar saints from the 6th–9th centuries CE. It is one of the 108 Divya Desams dedicated to Vishnu, who is worshipped as Saranathan and his consort Lakshmi as Saranayaki.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Koneswarar Temple, Kudavasal?
faq.a: The best time to visit Koneswarar Temple, Kudavasal is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Koneswarar Temple, Kudavasal?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Tiruchirappalli International Airport (85 km) and the nearest railway station is Tiruchirappalli Junction (87 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Koneswarar Temple, Kudavasal?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Koneswarar Temple, Kudavasal at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Koneswarar Temple, Kudavasal?
faq.a: Stays in Koneswarar Temple, Kudavasal start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Koneswarar Temple, Kudavasal?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kudavasal taluk, Kudavasal, Kudavasal block, Thirumeignanam Gnanaparameswarar Temple, Adippuliyur.
